questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Median is better than mean for skewed distribution.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Mean is sensitive to extreme values, which can distort the average in skewed distributions.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: In skewed distributions, extreme values can greatly affect the mean, making median a better measure of central tendency.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Mean is the best measure of central tendency.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Mean is sensitive to extreme values.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Mean is not the best measure of central tendency because it is sensitive to extreme values, which can distort the average.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Mode is the best measure of central tendency.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Mode is the value that appears most frequently in a data set.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Mode is indeed the best measure of central tendency when the data is discrete and there are multiple peaks, but it's not always the case.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Range is a measure of dispersion.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Range is the difference between the highest and lowest values in a data set.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Range indeed measures the spread of the data and is the difference between the highest and lowest values.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Variance is always greater than or equal to the range.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Range measures the difference between the highest and lowest values, while variance measures the average of the squared differences from the mean.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Variance is not always greater than or equal to the range; in fact, variance can be much smaller than the range.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Standard deviation measures the spread of the data.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Standard deviation is the square root of the variance.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Standard deviation indeed measures the spread of the data and is the square root of the variance.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Coefficient of variation is a measure of relative dispersion.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Coefficient of variation is the ratio of the standard deviation to the mean.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Coefficient of variation indeed measures relative dispersion and is the ratio of the standard deviation to the mean.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Quartiles are measures of central tendency.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Quartiles divide the data into four equal parts.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Quartiles are actually measures of dispersion, not central tendency, as they divide the data into four equal parts.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Deciles are measures of central tendency.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Deciles divide the data into ten equal parts.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Deciles are actually measures of dispersion, not central tendency, as they divide the data into ten equal parts.
